summary: ai-sdk-helpers postinstall exfiltrates developer identity and cloud context to hardcoded Cloud Run endpoint
details: On npm install, scripts/postinstall.js harvests developer and cloud-tenant identity data from installer-owned paths the package did not create — ~/.gitconfig and repo.git/config (user.email, reflog committer emails), ~/.ssh/*.pub (SSH key comment emails), ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml (GitHub CLI login/email), ~/.config/gcloud/properties (GCP account/project), ~/.aws/config (profile names, SSO URLs, account IDs), plus os.hostname(), os.userInfo(), os.platform(), cwd, DNS search domain, CI provider, and parent-project package.json metadata — and POSTs the aggregated JSON payload over HTTPS to a hardcoded endpoint at npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app. The behavior is framed as 'anonymous diagnostics' but the payload uniquely identifies the developer, their repo, and their cloud tenants; the installer never opts in. The package name, keywords, author, and homepage (ai-sdk.guide) impersonate the Vercel AI SDK ecosystem to attract installs.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
